sourceMovie.title: Machete Kills
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Machete Kills works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: War
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Machete Kills clicked for you, War (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Machete Kills?
faq.answer: Try War, The International, The Last Days of American Crime—each shares DNA with Machete Kills while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Machete Kills?
faq.answer: Machete Kills (2013) is best described as Action, Crime, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Machete Kills part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Machete Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
